epitaph: Once hailed as the hackable framework for a decade, it died mid-migration with an unfinished plugin system, a stalled 2.x release, and a final commit named 'final-cleanup' that was anything but final for the 48,213 stars left behind.

causes.label: Deliberate archival by owner — development formally ceased
Commit velocity collapse — 41 commits/month to 1 in six months
Unfinished plugin system and stalled 2.x release left the codebase incomplete
Unresolved bug accumulation with zero maintainer engagement

revival.step: Unarchive the repository and publish a clear governance document naming at least one active maintainer with merge authority
Triage and close or label all 14 open issues; address the stale Apple Silicon and memory-leak bugs first as they block basic usability
Finish or formally remove the plugin API — resolve the FIXME in src/plugins/index.ts and document the contract so the wave of 'pluggable X' feature requests can be evaluated
Complete the cache layer wiring (src/cache/store.ts) and replace the stopgap scheduler (src/core/scheduler.ts) to deliver on the 2.x promise
Publish a 2.0 release with a migration guide and deprecation timeline so the 6,172 forks have a canonical upgrade path

lastWordsGloss: 'final-cleanup' — because nothing says done like merging a PR called final into an archive and walking away from 48,213 believers without so much as a changelog entry.
